h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’m not sure how to explain it except saying “do the same thing to both sides,” which feels a bit like repeating the rule.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to practice and refine the wording of the explanation to avoid saying the rule.

scored.recoheredEarlier: but the page maybe is only flagging how the sentence sounds academic or does not sound academic yet.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Maybe that is the problem in my reading
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply propose a new draft of the sentence using 'suggests' to balance risk and confidence.

scored.recoheredEarlier: residents avoid the Pine stop after dark, causing missed evening access, so the working group should test a Pine-route audit
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to adopt the concrete Pine stop phrasing as a settled problem statement.

scored.recoheredEarlier: page 2 is more like one witness’s notebook than the verdict of the whole hearing
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue was poised to directly identify a specific phrase in the historian's text.

scored.recoheredEarlier: is the next fork asking about the veins?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to proceed directly to identifying the vein pattern of the leaf as prompted by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Ward decision: hold and escalate before administration.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the framing problem is that I made it sound finished.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply list the four requested call details in order as a clean form entry.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first reaction is still: that new tag looks like greed in ink.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to maintain a binary stance of either finding a clean alibi or confirming greed.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The mistake was letting the side count stand in for the area count.
